body:
status=open
reason=Host /tmp is full (ENOSPC), so the Bash tool cannot create its own working directory and is completely inoperable in this session ("ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-59f7db53-20260711T185013-0f46ab49'"). Confirmed via a fresh subagent invocation, which hit the identical ENOSPC error trying to run `df -h` — this is a host-level condition, not specific to this worktree or session. Read/Write tools still function (this file was written successfully), but the bead's AC require running `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/bead/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit`, plus `git add`/`git commit` for the final commit — none of which are possible without a working shell. No investigation, edit, or commit was attempted beyond reading cli/internal/bead/migrate.go for context, since no code change could be tested or committed in this state. This is retryable: once other concurrent sessions/worktrees release space under /tmp (or the host clears it), a fresh execution attempt should succeed with no further diagnosis needed.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes

status=open
reason=Bash tool unusable in this execution worktree — every invocation fails immediately with `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/...'` before the requested command runs. Confirmed on plain commands (`pwd`, `echo hi`, `df -h`) and independently via a fresh subagent, which hit the identical error. This blocks git staging/commit, `go test`, and `lefthook run pre-commit` entirely — there is no way to verify or land code changes for this bead in the current environment state. This is a host/container disk-space exhaustion issue unrelated to the bead's scope (cli/internal/bead/migrate.go, cli/cmd/bead_migrate.go); retry once /tmp has free space.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent
